dataset: PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Database (EEGMMIDB) v1.0.0

pi_items.source: install_openbci.sh converts PhysioNet S001R01.edf to this filename; setup_task.sh references the file at /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings/OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt
setup_task.sh creates directory /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots; export_result.sh and task.json specify exact path
verifier.py VLM prompt evaluates FFT X-axis ending ~40Hz; OpenBCI GUI docs confirm FFT Plot has Max Freq dropdown; with 250Hz sampling (Nyquist=125Hz), 40Hz is valid and reasonable for Alpha analysis
Wikipedia, ScienceDirect, and NCBI Bookshelf confirm Alpha rhythm is 8-12 Hz neural oscillations in EEG, standard neuroscience definition
install_openbci.sh downloads from this URL; PhysioNet documentation confirms S001R01 is eyes open baseline from 109-subject Motor Movement/Imagery study
PhysioNet EEGMMIDB documentation: '64 EEG signals, each sampled at 160 samples per second'

pi_items.verified_value: 8 channels, 250 Hz sampling, 120 second duration max
pi_items.source: convert_eeg_to_openbci.py: resamples to 250Hz, selects 8 channels (AF3/AF4, C3/C4, P7/P8, O1/O2), limits to 120 seconds

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real EEG data from PhysioNet EEGMMIDB v1.0.0, Subject S001 Run R01 (eyes open baseline). Original data has 64 channels sampled at 160 Hz. After conversion to OpenBCI format, it contains 8 channels spanning frontal-to-occipital regions, resampled to 250 Hz, and truncated to 120 seconds. Alpha Rhythm is correctly defined as 8-12 Hz. The data file 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt' is stored in /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings/. The target FFT max frequency of 40 Hz encompasses the full Alpha band (8-12 Hz) plus Beta context (12-30 Hz), with room to Nyquist limit (125 Hz at 250 Hz sampling).
